item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-040","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The answer rejects an even-handed 'both sides' synthesis and establishes a dominant judgment: old-code maintainability problems justify evidence-driven governance and usually gradual replacement, not a whole-backend rewrite. It demotes rewrite and anti-rewrite absolutism into constraints, names decision evidence and migration controls, and treats full rewrite as a last option under specific conditions. This satisfies the aspect-arbitration expectation without collapsing into balanced mush."}
